headline: The smallest nation ever scored at a World Cup — then Germany answered with SEVEN. Germany 7-1 Curaçao.
summary: Curaçao — the SMALLEST nation ever to reach a men's World Cup (population ~156,000, ~444 km²), who qualified UNDEFEATED out of CONCACAF under ~78-year-old Dick Advocaat — played its first-ever World Cup match in the Group E opener vs Germany at NRG Stadium, Houston, on 14 June 2026. RESULT (FINAL): Germany 7-1 Curaçao. Livano Comenencia scored Curaçao's FIRST-EVER World Cup goal to briefly make it 1-1 (a counter/deflection past Manuel Neuer) before Germany scored six unanswered. Kai Havertz bagged a brace (one penalty); Jamal Musiala, Nico Schlotterbeck, Nathaniel Brown and Deniz Undav also scored. The 7-1 scoreline echoes Germany's famous 7-1 demolition of Brazil at the 2014 World Cup. The story for Curaçao is that they were there at all — and that they put one past a four-time world champion.

curacao_scorers: Livano Comenencia (Curaçao's first-ever WC goal — briefly 1-1)

angles: A town of 156,000 people scored at a WORLD CUP before Germany buried them 7-1 — and that goal is the whole story.
Livano Comenencia: the man who scored the smallest nation in history's first-ever World Cup goal — past Manuel Neuer.
Germany 7-1. Where have we heard that before? (the 2014 Brazil echo) — Die Mannschaft sending a tournament warning.
They got there UNDEFEATED with a 78-year-old coach, lost 7-1, and STILL made history. Curaçao's day, told straight.
1-1 for about a minute: the briefest, biggest lead the smallest nation ever held at a World Cup.

Comenencia goal minute and whether it was the equalizer that made it exactly 1-1 (sources say yes, brief 1-1).
Dick Advocaat 'oldest manager at a World Cup' superlative — likely but confirm vs Otto Rehhagel (2010, ~71) etc. before asserting.
